content.problemStatement:
You are given a linked list with NNN nodes. You have to perform following commands -


Make groups of size MMM from the head node to the last node.
Assuming xxx groups are created, you have to reverse the order of nodes in each group.
Relative order of the groups in the final linked list must remain the same i.e after reversing, all the elements of group 111 should appear before group 222 and so on.
In order to solve this problem, you just have to complete the function

Node* reverseMSizeGroups(Node* head, int M) by returning the head node of the linked list

It is guaranteed that NNN is divisible by MMM for all test cases.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, the number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
The first line of each test case contains 2 integers NNN - length of linked list and MMM-group size
Second line of each test case contains NNN space separated integers val1,val2,....vali,...valnval_1, val_2,.... val_i,... val_nval1​,val2​,....vali​,...valn​ where valival_ivali​ is the value stored at ith node starting from the head node.

**Note:**



For C++ language, you need to:

Complete the function in the submit solution tab:


`Node* reverseMSizeGroups(Node* head, int M)
`
 \  



Output Format
Using the function you complete, for each testcase linked list generated must be the required linked list.



For each test case N space separated integers nval1,nval2,..,nvali,...nvalNnval_1, nval_2, .., nval_i , ... nval_Nnval1​,nval2​,..,nvali​,...nvalN​ will be outputted, where nvalinval_invali​ is the new value stored at ith node starting from the head node.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases ≤ 10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains two integers N and M — the length of the linked list and the group size. The second line contains N space‑separated integers representing the node values from head to tail.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with N space‑separated integers – the values of the linked list after reversing every consecutive group of size M while keeping the groups in their original order.

sampleTestCases.explanation: First test case groups of 3 are reversed: (1,2,3)->3 2 1, (4,5,6)->6 5 4, (7,8,9)->9 8 7. Second test case groups of 2 are reversed accordingly.

solution.approach: Traverse the list while counting nodes. For each group of M nodes, reverse the pointers using the classic in‑place reversal technique (prev, curr, next). Keep track of the previous group's tail to connect it with the newly reversed group and also store the new head after the first reversal. Because N is divisible by M, every node belongs to exactly one group. The algorithm runs in O(N) time and O(1) extra space per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the length of the linked list.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) auxiliary space (the list nodes are rearranged in place).

code.Python3:
import sys

def reverse_groups(head, M):
    # head is a list of values; we will simulate linked list reversal by slicing
    n = len(head)
    res = []
    for i in range(0, n, M):
        res.extend(reversed(head[i:i+M]))
    return res

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it)); M = int(next(it))
        arr = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(N)]
        res = reverse_groups(arr, M)
        out_lines.append(' '.join(map(str, res)))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
